mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in INDPRO signal expanding manufacturing activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month increases in INDPRO do not coincide with positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in at least 58% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE sustained above 8.0% reflects structural labor market weakness that depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, generating negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: UNRATE above 7.5% combined with month-over-month increases in INDPRO signals a recovery phase where firms expand output without commensurate wage pressure, expanding profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition of UNRATE > 7.5% and month-over-month INDPRO increase fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: HOUST above 600 thousand units signals a recovering residential construction sector that drives demand for building materials, employment, and credit expansion, producing positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods with HOUST above 600k do not generate positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns in at least 60% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS held below 0.25% forces investors out of money market instruments and into risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns as excess liquidity seeks higher yields.
failure_condition: If months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% do not produce positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns in at least 58% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: INDPRO sustained above an index level of 95 signals that manufacturing capacity utilization has recovered sufficiently to support durable corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If periods with INDPRO above 95 fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 58% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS signal expanding labor income and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 5-day forward returns as market participants reprice growth expectations.
failure_condition: If month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are followed by negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 40% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in GDPC1 signals broad-based economic expansion that supports sustained corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If positive quarterly changes in GDPC1 are not followed by positive S&P 500 60-day forward returns in at least 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
